title: Source-to-Package Release, Incident Command, and Production Defense · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds defensible production TypeScript feed aggregator from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of defensible production TypeScript feed aggregator while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources through authorized discovery, bounded Fetch and XML, runtime normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage publisher overload, stale ingestion, SSRF, entity, sanitizer, search, pool, tenant, async-handle, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Release Provenance
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 8893 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend source-to-package provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risks; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Release Provenance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted javascript, image digest, sbom, signature, configuration, and deployment. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Release Provenance, keep Feed Ts Release Provenance connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
